summary: meteora-sdk postinstall harvests host info and filesystem data over HTTP
details: The package's postinstall.js executes automatically on npm install and combines host reconnaissance (os.hostname(), os.userInfo(), os.homedir()) with filesystem enumeration (multiple fs.existsSync() probes against paths derived from the user's home directory) and outbound network I/O via the http module. The behavior fires at install time without user interaction, targets installer-owned identity and filesystem data unrelated to any legitimate SDK function, and traced content matched credential/system-info exfiltration patterns strongly enough to trigger the model provider's malware output filter. The package name suggests a Solana/Meteora DEX SDK, but no SDK functionality is required at install time — the postinstall script's sole observable purpose is data collection and off-host transmission.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
